architecture.summary: SAE-RSV refines steering vectors learned from small datasets by leveraging a pretrained Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) to identify and remove task-irrelevant noising features, then retrieves missing task-relevant features based on semantic similarity. The system operates on hidden activations from Llama-3-8B-Instruct (layer 25, 65K-dim SAE space), using an LLM (GPT-4o-mini) to judge feature semantics and construct textual explanations. Refined steering vectors are injected into the residual stream at inference time via a linear combination of the original steering vector, a noise-subtraction term, and an augmentation term.

biblio.title: Enhancing LLM Steering through Sparse Autoencoder-Based Vector Refinement

claims.dataset: five behavioral concepts (Sycophancy, Corrigibility, Hallucination, Myopic Reward, Survival Instinct)
claims.statement: SAE-RSV achieves superior performance across all five datasets, with success rate consistently surpassing all baselines; outperforms original prompt by average of 18.8%
SAE-RSV improves success rate by over 10% compared to CAA on four out of five datasets
SAE-RSV yields substantially higher success rates across all tasks compared to LoRA-SFT (supervised fine-tuning)

claims.dataset: five behavioral concepts (small training dataset setting, 50 sample pairs)
claims.statement: Over 93.6% of features captured by original steering vectors are noisy features; only 42.2% of all task-relevant features are captured by original steering vectors
Augmentation module yields average 7.2% increase in success rate (from 45.6% to 52.8%); Denoising module yields average 3.6% increase (from 45.6% to 49.2%)
Optimal steering performance achieved with 15–20 task-relevant features; CAA captures less than half of all useful features (7.6 vs 18.0 features on average)
With only 10 training samples, SAE-RSV matches the performance of CAA trained on 1000 samples

Denoising module reduces entropy (generation diversity/fluency) by 0.11; overly aggressive filtering collapses model hidden representation distributions into a single direction, leading to more repetitive text generations
